thesis: VYX is a deeply undervalued fintech asset transitioning from hardware to software and recurring payments revenue. Its massive installed base of banking and commerce technology creates high switching costs and sticky recurring revenue. Successful deleveraging and margin expansion could drive significant re-rating.

drivers: Faster software revenue growth outpacing hardware decline
Successful cost restructuring yielding $100M+ in annualized savings
Debt refinancing that materially reduces interest expense
Potential M&A interest from strategic buyers at a premium

assumptions: Modest revenue stabilization with software growth offsetting hardware decline
100bps gross margin expansion from mix shift
Steady debt paydown from improving free cash flow

risks: Hardware revenue collapses faster than software revenue grows
Debt covenant stress or unfavorable refinancing terms
Failed restructuring leading to further margin erosion

catalysts.event: Q2/Q3 earnings showing software revenue acceleration
New SaaS product launches in digital banking
Strategic partnership or acquisition in payments
Restructuring completion and cost savings realization
Potential M&A from strategic or financial buyers

key_risks: High debt load (~$2.5B) creates interest burden and covenant risk
Restructuring execution risk — complex cost-out and business transition
Faster-than-expected hardware decline offsetting software growth
Competition from fintech disruptors and incumbent processors
Customer concentration and negotiating power of large bank/retail clients
Higher-for-longer rates increasing interest expense and reducing bank IT spending
